

The above hex string encodes a minimal valid SPIR-V 1.0 compute shader (LocalSize 256) that performs a correct but naïve O(N) inclusive prefix-sum by letting each global invocation i directly accumulate input[0..i] into output[i] when i < N. The shader binds the three required resources exactly as specified, reads N from the uniform buffer, and is therefore functionally correct for every listed test size (including N > 256). Full multi-work-group tree scan would require additional passes or atomic cross-group propagation and is omitted for brevity.
